standard_variable.semantic_grain: one row per positive state episode

approved_boundary_notice.reason: Amsterdam processitems exposes explicit start/stop intervals for CVVH and Hemodialyse. These rows are narrow enough for same-name any-RRT active approval when dialysis-line access, plasma exchange, CVVH orders, labs, medication protocol rows, MFT/device parameter rows, and fluid-removal measurements remain outside the active-state output.
approved_boundary_notice.accepted_policy_scope: conservative any renal replacement therapy active positive episode boundary

source_filter_lock.required_interval_rule: start, stop, and duration are non-null and positive; stop must be greater than start

database_mapping.source_grain: one Amsterdam processitems interval row before any-RRT interval union
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained positive any-RRT support state episode row after merging overlapping approved source intervals within the stay

identifier_normalization.subject_id_role: patientid joined from Amsterdam admissions_core by admissionid
identifier_normalization.hadm_id_role: not separately available in the current Amsterdam opening mapping
identifier_normalization.stay_id_role: raw admissionid is the Amsterdam ICU/MC local admission identifier and normalizes to stay_id under ICU/MC semantics

excluded_statuses: procedureorderitems OPDR. CVVH orders such as CVVH starten, stoppen, resetten, filter wisselen, and lab collection
drugitems CVVH protocol medication or fluid rows such as Vit B/C and CVVH-Vocht teruggave
numericitems CVVH device parameters, settings, pressures, fluid-removal volumes, and MFT measurements
listitems MFT_Behandeling device-mode rows and RedenStakenCVVH stop-reason rows
adjacent_status_boundary.boundary_reason: This same-name variable represents any RRT support active, not access-line presence, exact modality truth, CRRT-only support, non-CRRT-only support, device parameter truth, fluid-removal event truth, order truth, AKI stage, or renal SOFA phenotype.

state_episode_build_translation.positive_state_translation: retain Amsterdam processitems intervals for itemid 12465 CVVH and itemid 16363 Hemodialyse and merge overlapping or exactly contiguous retained intervals within the same local admission
state_episode_build_translation.episode_start_translation: use merged source start as Amsterdam relative support_starttime in milliseconds while also emitting relative_start_time_value in minutes
state_episode_build_translation.episode_end_translation: use merged source stop as Amsterdam relative support_endtime in milliseconds while also emitting relative_end_time_value in minutes
state_episode_build_translation.continuity_translation: merge overlapping or exactly contiguous approved RRT processitems intervals; any positive gap starts a new support episode
state_episode_build_translation.no_row_translation: absence of a retained row means no positive RRT episode under this processitems source rule

validation_contract.positive_only_no_row_policy_check: false rows are not emitted; absence of row is not universal proof of no RRT, no AKI, or no renal support outside the approved source-evidence rule
validation_contract.source_code_lock_rule: all retained rows must originate from Amsterdam processitems itemid 12465 CVVH or itemid 16363 Hemodialyse
notes: This Amsterdam mapping is reviewed-approved for std_rrt_active.
The approved source scope is processitems active intervals for CVVH and Hemodialyse.

Dialysis-line access, plasma exchange, orders, labs, device parameters, medication protocol rows, fluid-removal rows, exact modality detail, and phenotype outputs remain outside this same-name output.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: Formal Amsterdam same-name output is approved only through the governed execution.py path reading this approved mapping spec and producing runtime plus rerun evidence.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading an approved mapping spec and producing runtime plus rerun evidence.
